summary: Crisp, clean SBD capture of a high‑energy 1983 show with Phil’s bass leading the band and multiple standout jams.
review: The five‑star reviews rave about this soundboard recording’s crystal‑clear mix (with only occasional crackle) and praise an explosive first set followed by a uniquely uptempo, jam‑heavy second set. Highlights include a blazing China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider, a monster The Other One, and Phil Lesh’s commanding bass work, while Jerry Garcia’s guitar is described as “charging” and Brent Mydland’s keyboards add extra sparkle. Overall the performance is deemed a hidden gem from what many consider the peak of the 1983 tour.

ai_rating.rationale: All five reviews give 5‑star ratings, consistently praising both the musical performance and the soundboard quality; no negative comments were found.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Described as crisp, clean and superlative with only minor crackle/hiss in spots; a few reviewers note the mix could use slight EQ tweaks but overall the SBD is superb.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
Beat It on Down the Line
show_quality.setlist_flow: Explosive, uptempo first set followed by a uniquely cohesive and high‑energy second set; the concert is praised for its seamless transitions and overall pacing.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s playing is described as “charging,” “tearing through Big Railroad,” and delivering manic flurries on Cumberland.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil Lesh receives repeated acclaim for leading the band, providing rumbling mastery, and being front‑and‑center throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland is highlighted for his standout moments (Dixie, Saturday Night) and overall keyboard brilliance.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey Hart is noted as slightly rigid and overbearing; no specific comment on Bill Kreutzmann.
